title: hieracium caesioides subsp. supraglaucum
intro: Hieracium caesioides subsp. supraglaucum, commonly known as the rough hawk's-beard, is a plant species in the dandelion family that is native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, making it a potential treatment for various health issues. The health benefits of Hieracium caesioides subsp. supraglaucum include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and boost the immune system. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
